introText: Compare Medicare Advantage, Medigap, and Part D prescription drug plans available in Alabama for 2026. Find the coverage that fits your health care needs and budget.

enrollmentPeriods.name: Initial Enrollment Period
enrollmentPeriods.description: Sign up for Medicare Parts A and B

enrollmentPeriods.name: Annual Enrollment Period
enrollmentPeriods.description: Switch Medicare Advantage or Part D plans

enrollmentPeriods.name: Medicare Advantage Open Enrollment
enrollmentPeriods.description: Switch MA plans or return to Original Medicare

specialPrograms.name: Alabama SHIP Program
specialPrograms.description: Free Medicare counseling and assistance for Alabama residents. Get help comparing plans, understanding benefits, and resolving billing issues.

specialPrograms.name: Medicare Savings Programs
specialPrograms.description: Low-income Alabama residents may qualify for help paying Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copayments.

localResources.name: Alabama Department of Insurance
localResources.description: Contact Alabama's insurance department for help with Medicare plan complaints and questions.

faqs.question: How many Medicare Advantage plans are available in Alabama?
faqs.answer: Alabama residents can choose from dozens of Medicare Advantage plans. Plan availability varies by county, so use Medicare.gov's Plan Finder to see options in your specific area.

faqs.question: When can I enroll in Medicare in Alabama?
faqs.answer: Most people first become eligible at age 65. Your Initial Enrollment Period is a 7-month window around your 65th birthday. You can also make changes during the Annual Enrollment Period (October 15 – December 7).

faqs.question: Does Alabama have a Medicare Savings Program?
faqs.answer: Yes. If you have limited income, Alabama may help pay your Medicare premiums and cost-sharing through Medicare Savings Programs. Contact your local Medicaid office for details.

faqs.question: How many Medicare Advantage plans are available in Cheyenne?
faqs.answer: Cheyenne residents can choose from multiple Medicare Advantage plans. Plan availability varies by county, so use Medicare.gov Plan Finder to see options in your area.

faqs.question: When can I enroll in Medicare in Cheyenne?
faqs.answer: Most people first become eligible at age 65. Your Initial Enrollment Period is a 7-month window around your 65th birthday.
